IPC 499.title: Definition of defamation
IPC 499.text: Whoever, by words either spoken or intended to be read, or by signs or by visible representations, makes or publishes any imputation concerning any person intending to harm, or knowing or having reason to believe that such imputation will harm, the reputation of such person, is said, except in the cases hereinafter excepted, to defame that person.
IPC 499.penalties: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine up to Rs. 5,000

IPC 500.title: Punishment for defamation
IPC 500.text: Whoever defames another shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
IPC 500.penalties: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine up to Rs. 5,000

Code of Wages 2019 Section 13.title: Overtime and compensation
Code of Wages 2019 Section 13.text: An employee working beyond normal working hours is entitled to overtime compensation at a rate higher than regular wages.

Industrial Disputes Act 1947 Section 23.title: Unfair labor practices
Industrial Disputes Act 1947 Section 23.text: Employers cannot terminate employees without due process or valid reason.

Employees State Insurance Act Section 45.title: Compensation for occupational disease
Employees State Insurance Act Section 45.text: Workers are entitled to compensation for work-related injuries and diseases.

Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 4.title: What may be transferred
Transfer of Property Act 1882 Section 4.text: All kinds of property can be transferred except immovable property outside India and property in trust.

Indian Succession Act 1925 Section 3.title: Application of act
Indian Succession Act 1925 Section 3.text: Governs succession to property of deceased persons in India.

IPC 447.title: Punishment for criminal trespass
IPC 447.text: Whoever enters into or upon property knowing that he is not entitled to do so shall be punished.
IPC 447.penalties: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs. 250

Information Technology Act 2000 Section.title: Hacking
Information Technology Act 2000 Section.text: Whoever with the intention of causing or knowing that he is likely to cause wrongful loss or damage to the public or any person accesses a computer resource without permission shall be punished.
Information Technology Act 2000 Section.penalties: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine up to Rs. 5 lakhs

Information Technology Act 2000 Section.title: Publishing obscene material
Information Technology Act 2000 Section.text: Whoever publishes or transmits obscene material electronically shall be punished.
Information Technology Act 2000 Section.penalties: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine up to Rs. 5 lakhs

Hindu Marriage Act 1955 Section 13.title: Grounds for divorce
Hindu Marriage Act 1955 Section 13.text: Either party to a marriage may present a petition for divorce on specific grounds.

Dowry Prohibition Act 1961 Section 3.title: Prohibition of dowry
Dowry Prohibition Act 1961 Section 3.text: It is unlawful to give or take dowry in any form.
Dowry Prohibition Act 1961 Section 3.penalties: Imprisonment up to 5 years or fine up to Rs. 15,000

IPC 498A.title: Cruelty by husband or his relatives
IPC 498A.text: Whoever, being a husband or the relative of a husband of a woman, subjects such woman to cruelty shall be punished.
IPC 498A.penalties: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine up to Rs. 100,000

IPC 302.title: Punishment for murder
IPC 302.text: Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death or life imprisonment and shall also be liable to fine.

IPC 304.title: Punishment for culpable homicide
IPC 304.text: Whoever commits culpable homicide not amounting to murder shall be punished with life imprisonment or imprisonment for a term up to 8 years.
IPC 304.penalties: Imprisonment up to 8 years or fine

Indian Contract Act 1872 Section 2.title: Definitions
Indian Contract Act 1872 Section 2.text: An agreement enforceable by law is a contract. Essential elements: offer, acceptance, consideration, intention, capacity.
Indian Contract Act 1872 Section 2.penalties: Contract can be voided if essentials missing

Indian Contract Act 1872 Section 73.title: Compensation for breach
Indian Contract Act 1872 Section 73.text: When a party breaches a contract, the other party is entitled to compensation.

Consumer Protection Act 2019 Section 2.title: Definitions and rights
Consumer Protection Act 2019 Section 2.text: Consumers have right to safety, information, choice, and complaint redressal.

Consumer Protection Act 2019 Section 53.title: Compensation by District Commission
Consumer Protection Act 2019 Section 53.text: Consumer can claim compensation for defective products or services.

Indian Penal Code Torts Section 336.title: Act endangering life or personal safety
Indian Penal Code Torts Section 336.text: Whoever does any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life shall be punished.
Indian Penal Code Torts Section 336.penalties: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs. 250

Constitution Article 14.title: Equality before law
Constitution Article 14.text: The State shall not deny to any person equality before the law or the equal protection of the laws within the territory of India.
Constitution Article 14.penalties: State actions can be declared unconstitutional

Constitution Article 19.title: Protection of certain rights regarding freedom of speech, etc.
Constitution Article 19.text: All citizens shall have the right to freedom of speech and expression, to assemble peaceably, to form associations, and to move freely.
Constitution Article 19.penalties: State actions violating this can be challenged

Constitution Article 21.title: Protection of life and personal liberty
Constitution Article 21.text: No person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law.
Constitution Article 21.penalties: Violating actions declared null and void, compensations awarded
